mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 2.5% annually while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, markets price in aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 70% of periods meeting both inflation and rate thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer income, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 45% of payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production combined with falling retail sales indicate a synchronized supply-demand contraction that triggers recession risk pricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 60% of periods where both series decline simultaneously.
